methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Terrahaptix is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Nathan Nwachuku and Maxwell Maduka that has raised $34M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 10 peers (2 public, 8 private), including same-stage benchmarks Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $34M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields $68M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M. This reflects the significant dispersion in how the Defense & Security sector values early-stage companies at this stage.

The two methods produce a wide spread, resulting in a triangulated range of $58M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $184M. The variance between stage-progression and peer-median outputs reflects tension between Terrahaptix's modest fundraising history relative to established competitors in the sector. Key uncertainties include the company's specific defensibility in Defense & Security applications, customer traction relative to peers, and whether the high end of peer valuations reflects technology differentiation or market momentum. A post-money series valuation of $120M–$220M would reconcile these approaches while acknowledging data constraints.
